titles.en: Chapter 128 – Limit of the walking during the Tawaaf
Chapter 128 – Limit of the walking during the Tawaaf

segments: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الطَّوَافِ فَقُلْتُ أُسْرِعُ وَ أُكْثِرُ أَوْ أُبْطِئُ قَالَ مَشْيٌ بَيْنَ الْمَشْيَيْنِ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الطَّوَافِ فَقُلْتُ أُسْرِعُ وَ أُكْثِرُ أَوْ أُبْطِئُ قَالَ مَشْيٌ بَيْنَ الْمَشْيَيْنِ .

segments: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Barqy, from Abdul Rahman Bin Sayaba who said,
‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the Tawaaf, so I said, ‘Quickly, or mostly (quickly), or slowly?’ Heasws said:’ Walk between the two walks (moderate pace)’.
en.hubeali.text: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Barqy, from Abdul Rahman Bin Sayaba who said, ‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the Tawaaf, so I said, ‘Quickly, or mostly (quickly), or slowly?’ Heasws said:’ Walk between the two walks (moderate pace)’.

segments: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Barqiy from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Sayabah who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the manner of walking during Tawaf around the Ka‘bah as to how fast or how slow should I walk. He (the Imam) replied, ‘Walk moderately in between the two kinds of walking.’” (Fatwa best explains the following chapters).
en.sarwar.text: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Barqiy from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Sayabah who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the manner of walking during Tawaf around the Ka‘bah as to how fast or how slow should I walk. He (the Imam) replied, ‘Walk moderately in between the two kinds of walking.’” (Fatwa best explains the following chapters).

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنِ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ سَيَابَةَ قَالَ
